mapping.a_element: three-wave interactions leading to kinetic equations for spectral densities
mapping.b_element: Smoluchowski aggregation equation with a source term leading to stationary mass distributions

mapping.a_element: energy and helicity spectra solutions of kinetic equations
mapping.b_element: Kolmogorov-type mass distributions carrying a constant flux of mass

mapping.a_element: Zakharov transformations used to analyze stationary solutions
mapping.b_element: Zakharov transformations used to derive stationary solutions

mapping.a_element: dependence of spectral constants on correlation between velocity and magnetic field
mapping.b_element: Kolmogorov constant being the same for a family of kernels

mapping.a_element: locality criterion for validity of spectra
mapping.b_element: locality criterion for admissibility of Kolmogorov spectrum

brief_justification: Both papers employ the method of Zakharov transformations to derive stationary solutions of kinetic equations describing flux-driven systems (energy/helicity spectra in MHD and mass distributions in aggregation). The underlying machinery involves wave/interaction-driven cascades with locality criteria and constant flux solutions, indicating shared mathematical structure.
